title: Rust and C test suites do not consume canonical test/vectors JSON (drift-invisible hardcoding)
description: Pattern across every domain: Python reads test/vectors/*.json directly (python/tests/test_vectors.py, runs in CI ci.yml:44); Rust and C transcribe the same values into hardcoded test literals (C's SCHC copy is explicitly sourced second-generation 'from rust/lichen-schc/src/codec.rs'). Editing a JSON vector fails no Rust/C CI job, so drift is invisible. Children cover each vector file plus the missing vector files. Project rule: all implementations MUST produce identical output for test/vectors/ - that requires actually loading them (or a codegen step with CI diff check).
